text.title: C# .NET Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns observed when scaling .NET clean-architecture solutions. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: Entities are bags of public-getters/public-setters; all business logic lives in `OrderService.Ship(order, ...)`. Invariants enforced inconsistently or not at all.
antipattern.root-cause: Authors treated entities as DTOs and put behaviour in services. Aggregate gives no protection.
antipattern.fix: Move `Ship`, `Cancel`, etc. onto the aggregate. Remove public setters. Service layer becomes coordination only (transactions, MediatR dispatch).

antipattern.symptom: Controller action method spans 50+ lines: validation, EF queries, aggregate construction, mapping. Tests require full integration host.
antipattern.root-cause: Controller skipped MediatR; persistence + business logic leaked into the transport layer.
antipattern.fix: Replace with a single `await _mediator.Send(new ShipOrderCommand(...))`. Controller becomes a thin dispatcher.

antipattern.symptom: `public Task<Order> Get(int id)` returns the EF entity. Swagger exposes `Discriminator`, `RowVersion`, navigation collections.
antipattern.root-cause: Entity reused as DTO; persistence shape leaked into public contract.
antipattern.fix: Project to `OrderResponse` record inside the query. Add explicit mapping in the handler or via Mapster/AutoMapper profile.

antipattern.symptom: AI-generated handler imports `MediatR.IRequestHandler<TRequest, TResponse>` from a non-existent namespace, or uses MediatR v9 API on a v12 project (`Handle(req, _) => ...` instead of `Handle(req, ct)`).

root-cause.source: LLM training corpus — outdated MediatR samples

antipattern.root-cause: Model regurgitated a mix of v9 + v12 API shapes.
antipattern.fix: Reject any handler that doesn't accept `CancellationToken`. Run `dotnet build` in CI to catch missing types. Pin MediatR major version in `Directory.Packages.props`.
